text:
Once upon a time in a quiet town named Willow Grove, there lived a shy squirrel named Theodore. Theodore loved exploring the forest, gathering nuts, and staring blankly at the flowers. One chilly autumn day, Theodore journeyed deeper into the arboreal wilderness.

Deep within stood an ancient oak tree. Legend had it that the oak tree concealed clues to a mysterious treasure. Thrilled, Theodore scampered up its rough bark. As he paused beneath its heavy shade, something clicked. He realized he couldn't leap anymore; he needed wisdom.

After a contemplative night under the stars, Theodore whispered quietly to himself.

"Begging your pardon?" an elderly owl whispered.

That's you!

"You prefer nuts?" the owl chuckled.

Theodore pondered.

"Yes... nuts."

With that resolve, he spent weeks collecting acorns. Months later, come springtime, he nestled under the old oak. On Easter Eve, his tiny voice echoed softly.

"Wiser?"

Inside rustled leaves!

"With cunning
